aids.title: Hash Table Components: ACH
aids.content: A = Array (storage), C = Conversion function (hash function maps key to index), H = Handler (collision resolution). Every hash table needs ACH.
aids.forRemembering: The three essential components of a hash table

aids.title: Hash Table as a Library
aids.content: A hash table is like a library where each book (value) is stored on a specific shelf (array index). The hash function is like the Dewey Decimal system that converts a book title (key) into a shelf number. Collisions are like two books assigned to the same shelf -- you need a plan to handle that.
aids.forRemembering: How hashing works conceptually with keys, indices, and collisions

aids.title: Load Factor Rule of Thumb
aids.content: alpha = n/m. Keep alpha below 0.7 for O(1) performance. High alpha means many collisions, which degrades to O(n) worst case. Remember: 70% full is the danger line.
aids.forRemembering: Load factor formula and its impact on performance
